text: Good morning, Jeremiah. We're operations section chief for the Palisades incident. We're going to give you a current situational update. Starting off with the acreage for the fire: twenty-three thousand seven hundred and seven acres. Current containment right at eleven percent. So we're going to start in the top into the map over here. We're going to call this the Maholan Drive up top. Crews did a really good job securing that, getting some containment line across the top, and fire was holding within the footprint yesterday. So we did have that northeastern wind that helped pull that fire back into the main body of the fire yesterday and gave some progress to the crews. One of our biggest concerns was the Mandeville Canyon yesterday. So we searched a lot of resources in there. The fire creeped down to Mandeville Canyon Road right behind the structures, and the crews were in there all night yesterday from the ground resources and the aerial show that was going on. Multiple helicopters were helping and the fixing. We had our VLATs, the very large air tankers, are in there assisting with that. The direct line was going in through the night. That is hand line directly on the fire line, and crews were doing well in there. Coming around down here in the Palisades area, across the PCH and into town, very limited calls for service last night. As you'll notice, the work of our crews in there mopping up, patrolling around all those structures, trying to hit every little hot spot. As the winds picked up today, we may see a little bit more activity in there. So we're trying to seek out a lot of those hot spots. We're able to use the IR capabilities from our UASs around and really focus on some of those hot spots and identify those for crews. Coming across down the PCH here, limited activity was happening yesterday. You notice we've continued to add some of this black line. That black line is the containment on the fire. So we're looking really good down here. Up here in the Montenegro area, crews actively got in there and went direct on some of the hand line.
